prestazioni: S50B36 (3.6L I6) engine upgraded with a custom Schrick 284° camshaft set (intake/exhaust) paired with Ferrea 6000-series titanium retainers and Supertech dual valve springs to handle 8,500 RPM safely, while maintaining OEM-like idle smoothness for the M-Tech II Touring's daily usability.

Full 3.8L stroker kit utilizing a forged Mahle Motorsport crankshaft (86.4mm stroke), Carrillo H-beam connecting rods, and custom 94mm Mahle pistons (9.5:1 CR) with Total Seal rings, mated to a ported and polished S50B36 head with oversized 33mm intake/30.5mm exhaust valves (Inconel) and a custom Schrick intake manifold for optimized airflow at 12.5 PSI via a Garrett GT3582R turbo (T4 divided .82 A/R) on a custom tubular manifold by TurboLab.

Full drivetrain reinforcement: 3.91:1 limited-slip differential from a Euro-spec E34 M5 3.8 (part #33101226526) with a 25% ramp angle, paired with a Sachs Performance twin-disc clutch (300mm) and a Getrag 420G gearbox (from E36 M3 GT) with a short-shift kit by UUC Motorwerks (SSK-E34) and a custom aluminum driveshaft with CV joints from a 540i Sport for durability under 550whp.
estetica: Full OEM+ resto-mod: Euro-spec M-Tech II Touring front bumper (part #51132226564) with integrated Hella 500 fog lights (clear lens) and a custom carbon fiber splitter by Vorsteiner (E34-specific, non-generic), retaining the factory tow hook for period-correct aesthetics.

Custom widebody conversion using genuine E34 M5 Sport (3.8) rear fenders (part #41122226528) with 20mm spacers (Hubcentric, 5x120) to fit staggered BBS RS-GT II wheels (18x9.5 ET15 front, 18x11 ET0 rear) wrapped in Michelin Pilot Sport Cup 2 tires (255/35/18 front, 295/30/18 rear) for aggressive stance without fender rub.

Full interior refresh with Recaro SRD (E34-specific mounting brackets) in Nappa leather (BMW Individual color #405 Hellgrau) with custom M-Tech II Touring door cards (retrimmed in Alcantara with red stitching), paired with a Momo Prototipo Evo steering wheel (380mm, suede) and a custom roll cage by Matter Fab (bolt-in, 4-point) painted in BMW Dakar Yellow (331) for track readiness.
